text: We have performed the standard three ports left butts with two five millimeter port one for the camera and another one for
an aiding instrument represented by an endo-grasp and a further 12mm port for the endoscopic needle driver.
After initial assessment of the left hemidiaphragm relaxation, a continuous suture was placed with the aid of the endoscopic suturing device.
We can see from the video how easy is the passage of the needle through one side and the other of the suture line and from one side to the other of the diaphragm.
The suture used is an absorbable barbed suture
unidirectional with a final lock system that voids the initial tying.
We can see from this video how the continuous suture performed is placed under tension throughout the procedure.
This video also shows how the aid of CO2 increases the space
available to the surgeon in order to perform the placation of the diaphragm as low as possible within the pleural cavity.
